entry.title: The fastest way to a deployable CAP app on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
So, you have developed your CAP app locally and are wondering: What's the fastest way to get this on the BTP?

Or, if you are like me, you sometimes want to try something out in a deployed version quickly.

Even if things are running smoothly in your local environment, correctly binding and bundling everything up into a working cloud app, that is connected to the database, handles authorization etc. can be cumbersome.

However, CAP has a few tools, that simplify this a lot.

For me the shortest way to get an application deployed is the following:

After logging on to cf using the cli with 

 

cf l --sso

 

I execute the following in the shell:

 

cds init fast_deploy
cd fast_deploy
cds add sample
cds add mta,hana,xsuaa --for production
cds add workzone
npm i
npm i --prefix app/admin-books
npm i --prefix app/browse
mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar
cf deploy gen/mta.tar

 

 Given that there is a working BTP subaccount configured with the correct entitlements as well as HANA Cloud and Workzone ready, this will:

Line1: Initialise a new empty CAP project

Line3: Add a sample application with domain model, service definition and two sample Fiori apps (if you are doing this in your own project you can as well just skip those initial three lines.)

Line 4+5: Let the cds dk add all the relevant configurations and specify

  • SAP HANA as the target database
  • xsuaa as the identity service
  • the `mta.yaml` file which describes how all of this should be deployed
  • SAP BTP Workzone as the entry page holding the tiles for your Fiori apps

Line 6-8: Installing the dependencies for the CAP app and the two Fiori apps.

Line 9: Building the MTA 

Line 10: Deploying it to Cloud Foundry

So, with 10 lines in the shell, we have created an app, added all dependencies, bundled it up and deployed it to the BTP.

All that is left is to configure the app to show up on the Workzone (updating the content channel, add the apps in the content manager and assign them to a role and a group) after which you can also access the Fiori apps.

And for getting rid of the application without manually removing service keys and instances, just use the following:

 

cf undeploy fast_deploy --delete-services --delete-service-keys

 

That's it. For any developer who ever spent a few hours trying to manually adjust configurations in yaml or json files while getting a new error message after each redeployment... this should help make life easier 😀

All the best

Daniel

entry.title: S_PROGNAM Authorization check always successful
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I activated the scenario BC_GENERIC_REPORT_START using transaction SACF, which enabled the authorization check for S_PROGNAM. When users attempt to run a critical program without the necessary authorization in S_PROGNAM, they receive an authorization warning (not an error!). Despite the warning message, after confirming it, they are still able to access the initial screen of the report.

The failed authorization checks related to these warnings are visible in SU53 or STAUTHTRACE. However, my goal is to prevent users from accessing the report altogether once they receive and confirming the warning message. Instead, they should be immediately redirected out of SA38. How can I implement that? Below are several screenshots for the issue I'm talking about.

When an end user tries to run a report for which they do not have the necessary authorization,

 they receive the following warning:

However, if they click on "Continue," they are still able to access the initial screen of the report:

I want to prevent this from happening. Instead, they should be immediately redirected out of SA38. Do you know how I can implement this?

Best Regards,

Alp

entry.title: Re: 'Free' text from classroom trainings registration
entry.content: @JyotiA, the "showPricingInternalUsers"/"showPricingExternalUsers" settings can be reviewed (KBA 2228338). Please note the following details available right above these settings in the "FINANCIAL" area: "When set to false, catalog search will not include currency filter or price sorting and prices will not be displayed in user catalog, user registration and available offerings. This configuration does not turn off commerce functionality and should not be set to false if any learning has a Price associated for the respective user group.". Alternatively, the "Free" text label value could be reviewed (KBA 2120336).

entry.title: SAP CAP Node.js - Insert vector embedding data into hana cloud is not working
entry.content:
Hello, everyone,

I referred these two links cap-ai-vector-engine-sample ,cap-llm-plugin-samples  to develop a CAP RAG application.

When I insert the embedding data into the db, I got an error message "argument type mismatch: Result is not a single value".

After my research, I found that if the dependency @SAP/cds-hana is added, data can be inserted into the database successfully, However, my App use Fiori elements and enbaled draft function, if the @SAP/cds-hana dependency is used, my Fiori app will encounter an error: SqlError: invalid column name: DRAFTADMINISTRATIVEDATA.INPROCESSBYUSER. When this dependency is removed, the Fiori app will not report an error, but I won't be able to insert data into the db. It seems that this is a dilemma, So, is there a conflict between @SAP/cds-hana and the draft-enabled function?  And is there any way to insert data into the db normally without using this dependency?

Any advice or suggestions would be appreciated.

schema.cds

entity DocumentChunk : cuid, managed {
    file            : Association to Files;
    text_chunk      : LargeString;
    metadata_column : LargeString;
    embedding       : Vector(1536);
}

service.js ( insert into db )

const embeddingResult = await capllmplugin.getEmbeddingWithConfig(
          embeddingModelConfig,
          chunk.pageContent
        );

embedding = embeddingResult?.data[0]?.embedding;

const entry = {
          file_ID: uuid,
          text_chunk: chunk.pageContent,
          metadata_column: fileName,
          embedding: array2VectorBuffer(embedding),
        };
textChunkEntries.push(entry);

// Insert the text chunk with embeddings into db
      const insertStatus = await INSERT.into(DocumentChunk).entries(
        textChunkEntries
      );

package.json

 "dependencies": {
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "3.25.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience": "3.25.0",
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4",
    "cap-llm-plugin": "^1.4.4",
    "express": "^4",
    "langchain": "^0.1.19",
  }

entry.title: Re: Settlement Document: Tax Code and Tax determination i...
entry.content:
Hi @Pankaj_M, 


To enable automatic tax code determination from pricing in manual settlement documents (0S32), you need to:

  1. Copy the standard Settlement Process Type and modify the configuration to change the relevant entry from "Manual Entry" to "3 - From Price Determination". This ensures that the system automatically picks up the tax code and amount from pricing.

  2. Alternative Approach – Transfer Manager

    • You can use Transfer Manager to default the tax code during settlement document creation.
    • If additional logic is required, you may need to implement custom logic to determine the tax code dynamically.

Since you are using Vertex for tax determination, ensure that it is properly integrated with SAP Settlement Management and can retrieve all relevant data for tax determination. Typically, Vertex uses fields like:

  • Ship-to Party
  • Sold-to Party

However, these fields do not exist in a Settlement Document by default. You may need to explore custom solutions to ensure that the required tax-relevant data is available to Vertex at the time of settlement.

Let me know if you need further clarification!

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Cost Center in TRM Financial Transactions
entry.content:
This article describes how to use cost center in financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) of in S/4HANA Public Cloud, mainly focus on how to derive the cost center in posting journals arising from TRM transactions.

1. When creating transactions for some product categories (600, 760, 620), a cost center can be entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab.

For example, below is a FX forward transaction (product category 600):

When doing so in the transactions of product category 600 or 760 (OTC FX option), the major purpose is using cost center as a differentiation criterion in hedge management to match the FX exposure and FX derivative at the level of cost center. For more details regarding differentiation criteria in hedge management, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/618b3d58eaa89144e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US.

The cost center entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab will not be reflected in the position indicator of this transaction, and consequently, the cost center will not be carried over to the posting journals generated from the transaction.

2. Through configuration activity 102639 (Manage Default Account Assignments), you can define the default cost centers for cost elements and company codes specifically, and the TRM posting journal line items for those cost elements will have the default cost centers assigned. Please note that the cost center assigned here is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions, because this configuration take effects during creation of postings in financial accounting.

This method is useful when you have TRM specific cost elements (such as charge, interest income/expense, valuation gain/loss, etc.), and it is reasonable to assign a single default cost center to the postings related to this cost element from multiple TRM transactions, ensuring consistency and simplifying the accounting process.

3. Through configuration activity 105341 (Allocate Additional Account Assignments to Account Assignment References), you can assign a cost center to a combination of company code and account assignment reference (AAR).

Afterwards, when creating a TRM transaction, the cost center can be derived from company code and AAR, and it is stored in the position indicator. Then in the posting journals arising from this transaction, this cost center will be assigned to the cost element related line items by default.

This method is useful when you have multiple AARs, and different AARs should derive their corresponding cost centers.

The level of detail in the cost centers derived using this method actually depends on how detailed the AARs are defined and the preconditions on which the AARs are based. For more details on the derivation of AARs, please refer to the help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/713cc111330d4439a7331df96d070e5b.html?locale=en-US.

4. In app Manage Substitution / Validation Rules - Journal Entries, you can create a substitution rule with the business context Coding Block. The preconditions in the substitution rule should be comprehensive enough in order to derive the correct cost center (otherwise it doesn’t have advantage compared to other means), and the preconditions might rely on other substitutions.

Additionally, implementing the BAdI FIN_CODING_BLOCK_SUBSTITUTION might be beneficial as it offers more flexible development extensibility, allowing for customized coding block substitutions to better meet complex business requirements. For details, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/6d95f6fa596646f59cfb9dd0dd7b7fd3.html?locale=en-US.

Please note that the cost center derived via substitution rule or BAdI implementation is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions, because they take effects during creation of postings in financial accounting.

5. Through configuration activity 104115 (Define and Assign Differentiations), you can define a differentiation which includes term Cost Center, and then assign this differentiation to proper valuation area(s) and/or accounting code(s).

Afterwards, when creating a financial transaction within the related accounting code or for related valuation area, in the tab Administr., you will see the field Cost Center within the subarea Position Assignment. And this Cost Center field is mandatory to be entered with a value.

The cost center entered in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab is stored in position indicator of this transaction, and consequently, the cost center will be carried over to the cost element related line items of the posting journals generated from this transaction. In the position indicator, the cost center column is grayed out, because it must be identical with the cost center specified in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab.

This method is useful when transaction (of a product category other than 600, 760, 620 which have cost center field opened in tab Administr. by default) requires separate and manual determination of the cost center and store it in tab Administr. of the transaction, and this cost center should be used to record the profit/loss arising from this transaction from accounting perspective.

However, the decision to use Define and Assign Differentiations should be made cautiously, as it not only makes the Cost Center visible in transactions but also has several significant impacts on transaction processing and accounting, which need to be thoroughly evaluated before configuration:

  • For every transaction within the valuation area and/or accounting code which is assigned with this differentiation, cost center field will be a mandatory field to entered in financial transactions.
  • Position and quantity of financial transactions will be split according to cost centers in addition to predefined differentiations, this effect is especially obvious for financial transactions and class positions of securities (e.g. bond, stock).
  • Because of above point, the profit/loss (for example, realized gain/loss when derecognition) will be measured and accounted in line with each cost center.

6. You may also manually specify a cost center in position indicator (when it is possible). This method can be used in rare circumstances when a different cost center than the one derived via other means is required due to specific exceptions or unique transaction characteristics.

entry.title: Re: Configuration of Quick Apply in Recruiting. Advantages and Disadvantages
entry.content:
Hi there!

We encountered a similar inquiry from our Talent org, re: candidate pool expansion via shortened application and were informed we could create an entirely new, shortened application template to attach to various areas and generate candidate pools from such. In our situation, we were unable to pursue the option for an amended app template available for different app interfaces (quick apply via various networking sites) because it failed to meet our compliance criteria threshold. We had required questions that in of themselves made the application longer, but couldn't be omitted. 

While the shortened app did assist with faster candidate initial application times, it did require an additional internal business process to handle responses and then engage based off quick apply results. This also required we route through full application for full data receipt, attribute manually to recommended/forwarded apply to requisitions and process from there via our business flow status etc within RCM. We elected not to pursue since the compliance reqs as a security attributed govt/federal contractor prohibited much shortening without a significant uptick in change to our existing candidate profile requirements for basic quals. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Solving RSA1 issue after BW Refresh
entry.content:
"First, we’ll delete all irrelevant entries for TABLE – RSBASIDOC, RSLOGSYSDEST, RSLOGSYSMAP"

Any kind of manual change or modification of RSBASIDOC is not supported. This table should never be touched manually. The only exception is if SAP (support, development) explicitly asks for it. It should never be something, to be done on a regular basis.

If a specific source system is not required anymore, then instead of removing it from RSBASIDOC, just delete the source system itself.

 

There are official documents, that provide detailed descriptions of BW Systemcopy procedures:

The main guide to perform a manual systemcopy with a consistent result is described in the following resources:

  • 886102 - System Landscape Copy for SAP NetWeaver BW
  • BW SystemCopy and SAPI Source System connections
  • 2176213 - Important SAP notes and KBAs for BW System Copy
  • 2572113 - BDLS jobs canceled, "Cannot copy transformation" error occurred
  • 2924111 - [BW Central KBA] BDLS
  • Myself connection
  • How to check a BW <-> SAP source system connection
  • BW SAPI Source Systems - Troubleshooting
  • 2956859 - [BW Central KBA] Troubleshooting SAPI Source Systems Inconsistencies
  • 2005372 - Error messages after system refresh procedure (RSAR, E0)
  • 2344235 - Consulting: Handling Data Archiving Processes during systemcopies

For a BW system with ODP source systems it is strongly recommended to use the Post Copy Automation Tool.

entry.title: Re: Inventory should be blocked at the delivery and not a...
entry.content:
Hi,

or course it depends on configuration like ATP scope of check and other settings in your system how exactly you experience the process, especially in OnPremise/Private Cloud where you have a lot of options when it comes to customizing and enhancements.

When batches are determined a simulative ATP check is executed. Once the batch is chosen, the batch information is persisted on delivery item level and hence, respected in concurrent ATP checks. However, there is a timeframe between triggering the batch determination and really choosing it, where concurrent check could influence the available quantity as it is not yet in the delivery item.

Another aspect is that in WM the batches of delivery might not be considered, depending on lived processes, as the warehouse efficiency is treated higher. This is, however, not a field for ATP then.

Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: SAP Test Automation – What tools are available?
entry.content:
Some of you are probably familiar with SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing (ECT) or also known using the name Tricentis Tosca. This tool is already on the market for several years and it is well known with the test automation community. It can be fully integrated with Solution Manger and there is also lite version available named “Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Solution Manager”. The lite version is still fairly powerful, and it comes at no cost for customers, that do have Enterprise Support agreements with SAP.

Some while ago SAP in partnership with Tricentis have issued a new cloud offering called “Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Cloud ALM”, I will refer to “TTA with CALM” in my blog to make it simpler. Some people also refer to this product as Tosca Cloud. But it isn’t the official naming used by neither SAP, neither Tricentis. This comes also free of charge for customers with Enterprise Support agreements. However, based on our experience this also comes without any costs to customers with Preferred Success and possibly to all customers using SAP Cloud ALM. However, I’m unable to verify this statement. And by the way SAP Cloud ALM is a very powerful tool and lot of our customers are unaware that they are eligible to use it free of charge as long as they have any Cloud product from SAP. I really recommend checking the eligibility with your SAP Account Executive.

Very recently SAP (I believe some time in 2024) SAP has also released a paid version called “SAP Test Automation by Tricentis (STA)”.

The below picture nicely depicts different product options and some high-level limitations.

 

 

 

Lot of customers are asking us for an advice on what tool do we recommend. Whether they should opt for the on-premise SAP ECT (Tosca) or they should go for SAP STA (Cloud Tosca) integrated with SAP Cloud ALM. Or their free of charge alternatives TTA

This isn’t really an easy question. However we have recently had a chance to work with the latest Cloud version, so I could provide my angle.

 

Firstly, I have to say that the cloud version is getting constant updates with every single quarter. And really, I believe SAP and Tricentis are investing a lot in order to make both tools comparable. The question we often get is, whether STA is on par with ECT. Well, a huge progress was recently made when STA & TTA with CALM also natively support automation of SAP GUI. Unfortunately, previously only automation of SAP Fiori was possible, which obviously had a bit of limited use for SAP customers, as many of them are in sort of hybrid phase and rely both on Fiori and GUI still.

 

Below picture illustrates STA roadmap in 2024 and beginning of 2025

 

 

If you need to decide on which tool you as organization want to use. You have to first understand what applications and process you want to test with test automation solution. Potentially there is also fifth SAP option and that is applicable for customers on S/4 HANA Public Cloud. If you run on S/4 HANA Public Cloud, then you can use so call „S4 HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool“.  Maybe at some point this offering will contain rich features, I believe at this stage it provides limited functions. It can be nicely connected with Post Upgrade Tests, which from my point of view is really nice. However the inability to test Side-by-Side extensions makes the usability extremely limited to core proceses.

 

 

So back to the original question on what tool to select. If your landscape is predominantly cloud based with web UIs, and you use or plan to utilize SAP Cloud ALM then simply go for the “STA or TTA integrated with SAP Cloud ALM”. If your goal is to also automate non-SAP applications and non-Web based UIs, then simply go for SAP ECT (Tricentis Tosca).

Probably it is worth to consider following aspects:

 

There are however some limitations for TTA/STA developers, but I will summarize them in a different blog post.

SAP Cloud ALM test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud SOLMAN Test Suite SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery

entry.title: How to attach FE OP Controller Extension function to OData V4 dataReceived event?
entry.content:
I want to catch the dataReceived event of OData V4 model which is used by a Fiori Elements Standard Object Page in a Controller Extension. So that after every time the data is loaded or refreshed on the screen, I get into my custom Controller Extension function.

At onAfterRendering the data is not yet available.
So I have to attach an event to the model.

When I tried this

 

onAfterRendering: function () {
  var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
  oModel.attachEvent('dataReceived', myFunction);
},

 

I saw that only events messageChange and sessionTimeout are available in the model.

So maybe they are available after the ExtensionAPIPromise, but it the function is not called.

 

onInit: function () {
  var oView = this.getView();
  ExtensionAPI.getExtensionAPIPromise(oView).then(
    function (oExtensionAPI) {
      oExtensionAPI.attachPageDataLoaded(function (event) {
        var sPath = event.context.sPath;  // get the path
        var oData = event.context.getModel().getProperty(sPath); 
      }.bind(this));
    }.bind(this)
  )
},

 

I also tried this. but the event is not triggered. Probably because it creates a new binding.

onAfterRendering: function () {
  var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
  var oContextBinding = oModel.bindContext("/Run");
  oContextBinding.attachEvent('dataReceived', function (event) { 
    console.log('Testing...')
  })
},

Could you help me?

entry.title: SAC - Advanced Formula Data Action - Calculate Prior Previous Year Ending Balances.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We are currently facing a challenge in SAP Analytics Cloud concerning data action using advanced formulas, specifically regarding the calculation of previous years' figures within the Account and Date dimensions. We are utilizing an Account-Based Model perform forecasts on our Balance Sheet Model.

 

Requirement:

Our objective is to calculate the Prior Year Average Balance for future periods based on actual data. The formula we would like to implement is as follows:

=(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)/2

 Example:

For example, when we are on **January 2025**, our calculation should reference the following values:

 

If I am in any month in any month of current year, it has to pick last year Dec Ending values of Accounts Payable data from Account Dimension – Prior Previous Year Dec Data for Account Payable data from Account Dimension.

 

Chart:

 

2023.Dec

2024.Jan

2024.Feb

2024.Mar

2024.Apr

2024.May

2024.Jun

2024.Jul

2024.Aug

2024.Sep

2024.Oct

2024.Nov

2024.Dec

2025.Jan

10

12

13

15

15

15

20

20

25

25

30

35

40

50 (Expected)

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

- **2024 Dec** (Current Year End Balance)

- **2023 Dec** (Prior Year End Balance)

 

Thus, the expected result for **2025 Jan** would be:

On Jan 2025 When I run data action it should calculate : =(PY-1 End Bal + PY End Bal)

2024.Dec + 2023

40               +    10  Expected Results = 50 on 2025.Jan

 

 

Current Approach:

At present, I am utilizing the `PREYEARLAST` function to retrieve last year's ending data. However, I am encountering difficulties in formulating a straightforward syntax to fetch the data from the Prior Previous Year.

 

To address this, I have implemented the following workaround, although I have concerns regarding its efficiency:

 

IF [d/Date].[p/MONTHDESC] = "Jan" THEN

  DATA([d/Account]="Stat_PYAvgBal_AP") =   (RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREYEARLAST([d/Date])) +

    RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account]="Stat_AP", [d/Date]=PREVIOUS(13, "MONTH", [d/Date]))) / 2

```

 

This approach retrieves data for all 12 months of the current year while going back 24 months, which may not be optimal.

 

Request for Assistance:

I would greatly appreciate any insights or recommendations on achieving this calculation in a more efficient manner. Your expertise in this area would be invaluable.

 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

 

Regards,

Ramesh

entry.title: Value Accelerator Event - Structure question
entry.content:
Dear Community, I am reaching out to you because I am trying to understand how one of the events that I have in a Value Accelerator.

This event is suppose to materialize the Supplier invoice due date. 

Here is the code associated : 

 

SELECT
c_caseid,
c_time,
c_eventname,
"Number of Invoice Line Items",
"Accounting Document Primary Key"
FROM (

SELECT
CONCAT(bseg_bkpf.mandt, bseg_bkpf.bukrs, bseg_bkpf.gjahr, bseg_bkpf.belnr) AS c_caseid,
CASE -- always adding 23:59:59 as time
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd3t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd2t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd2t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
WHEN COALESCE(zbd3t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd2t, 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(zbd1t, 0) > 0 THEN DATE_ADD('day', CAST(zbd1t AS INT) ,from_unixtime(zfbdt/1000 + 86399))
ELSE from_unixtime(zfbdt / 1000 + 86399)
END AS c_time,
'Due Date passed' AS c_eventname,
COUNT(bseg_bkpf.buzei) OVER (PARTITION BY bseg_bkpf.belnr) AS "Number of Invoice Line Items",
bseg_bkpf.bkpf_c_key AS "Accounting Document Primary Key"
FROM bseg_bkpf
WHERE zfbdt IS NOT NULL
AND (bseg_bkpf.tcode != 'MR8M' OR bseg_bkpf.tcode IS NULL)
AND bschl IN (
-- Invoices
'31', -- Vendor Invoices
'22', -- Reverse Invoice
'21', -- Credit Memos
'32' -- Reverse Credit Memo
)
)

WHERE c_eventname IS NOT NULL
AND c_caseid IS NOT NULL
AND c_time IS NOT NULL

 

What I am not sure to understand is that it seems we are adding a timestamp to the due date but I don't get why as if the invoice is due today : then it is easy to determine just using the date if the invoice is overdue or not. 

Any idea ???

Thank you

Sanddie

entry.title: SAP just launched the Business Suite - what does this mean for Finance?
entry.content:
Feb 13, 2025 is “Business Unleashed Day” for the SAP Community, with announcements on SAP Business Suite and SAP Business Data Cloud - and how AI, data, and applications are combined like never before to help you unleash your full potential and make you unstoppable.

Whether your company has been running an SAP ERP solution for many years, or whether you’re just at the beginning of the evaluation of a Cloud ERP solution, you might be wondering what impact these announcements – especially the announcements of the SAP Business Suite - will have on your landscape planning.

Evolution from monolithic to composable

Just as technology has completely changed since ERP was first defined for 1970s mainframes, SAP’s approach to ERP has evolved to keep up you keep us with the needs of your business:

  • Modular capabilities that can be adopted quickly as new needs arise
  • Always up-to-date software-as-a-service solutions that give you non-disruptive access to innovations
  • Clear interoperability and data semantics so that you will be in the best position to realize the benefits of AI

The 2025 Gartner® ERP Primer for 2025 states that “in Gartner’s 2023 ERP, Procurement, HCM and Finance Apps Survey of those manager level and above who are responsible for ERP decision making, 71% reported their organizations were either in process or have plans to adopt a flexible and agile ERP architecture within two years.”

So, chances are, you’re already thinking about the importance of having the flexibility to choose business technology more flexibly to support your growth initiatives.

 

 

Gartner: ERP Primer for 2025, by Denis Torii, Dixie John. GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and is used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

 

The SAP Business Suite is SAP’s answer to Composable ERP

You know SAP as a leader in Cloud ERP for years, but the “ERP and Finance” focus of finance teams may overlook the additional, incremental value SAP can provide with our broader family of solutions – solutions that address business needs and can be adopted incrementally, like Fieldglass, Ariba, SuccessFactors, Concur, and CX.

Since the interoperability and data semantics among the applications is curated by SAP, the insights that can be achieved with SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Business AI are limitless.

 

RISE with SAP is a transformation journey aimed at helping SAP ERP customers transition to the SAP Business Suite - so they can stay ahead of the competition by continuously innovating – across their whole enterprise landscape – not just in “ERP”.

What this means is that Finance teams should be thinking of the value that SAP solutions can create in your IT landscape. For example, adopting updated, world-class procurement processes have benefits specifically for the CFO in terms of cash visibility, risk mitigation and more. You can read a great blog on the topic here.

The SAP Business Suite approach encourages companies to think beyond their administrative ERP footprint: how can we adopt the best solutions to solve all types of business problems – for the Chief Revenue Officer, or for the Chief Human Resources Officer?

Cloud software and software-as-a-service promised that business teams would be able to pick-and-choose the services that meet their business requirements. However, often IT does have to be involved to manage integrations and ensure data interoperability. The SAP Business Suite aims to make that modularity - and business user choice - promise a reality.  

And modularity is not just part of the North Star architecture at the line of business level. SAP Business Suite is a reminder that within the line of business solutions, there are software-as-a-service solutions that can be adopted to solve specific business problems – even if your current core architecture might be more monolithic than you’d like.

Composability within Financial Management

Even within the areas that are traditionally considered to be “ERP”, SAP is offering more flexibility and modularity than ever before. Based on your business needs, these SaaS solutions – with their out-of-the-box best practices and constant access to innovation - are available as extensions to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  • SAP Green Ledger - to align your carbon and financial accounting by integrating carbon emissions with your business processes, complementing SAP’s portfolio of sustainability solutions, including SAP Sustainability Control Tower.
  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing – for orchestrating financial closing activities across ERP systems
  • SAP Account Substantiation and Automation by BlackLine – to automate manual closing tasks and reconciliations
  • SAP Digital Banking – to automate connections to any financial service provider (banks, SWIFT, payment service providers) in a quick and easy way for more control
  • SAP Cloud for Credit Integration – to integrate and manage external credit rating information for completely touchless, responsive credit decisionmaking
  • SAP Trading Platform Integration – to connect to leading trading platform providers for inbound and outbound straight-through-processing of trades 
  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management - to automate compliance checks and manage remediations efficiently

These solutions – that solve specific business problems- are integrated with our standard financial management processes in Cloud ERP.

So, if you’re part of the 70% of ERP leaders thinking about flexibility in ERP, you should consider what the SAP Business Suite can do for your organization. Making the most of the SAP Business Suite will help you

  • Manage uncertainty through effective steering and agile planning
  • Navigate transformation supported by efficient processes to balance growth and profitability
  • Enable compliance and sustainability in an ever-evolving regulatory and risk environment

Learn more about Finance solutions here.

entry.title: Re: International Editable SALV Day 2025...
entry.content:
I'm in love with CL_SALV and I make all efforts available to prevent users to ask for editable fields. And when they do, I usually add a hotspot in the cell that leads to a popup. CL_GUI did never deserved all the effort you must put in their events to prevent errors and control changes.

One of my biggest fears is to see "editability" implemented in CL_SALV_TABLE. Because they will implement it the same way they "improved" this site.

Yes, now you are able to edit those cells, but to grant you that, we had to prevent hotspots, colors and even custom status bars and functions. But don't worry, we are working hard to fix that, and we plan to release it just the day after the "follow" feature in our wonderful site.

Because they are SAP, and they care about things we cannot even fathom.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Understanding DIV and MOD in ABAP and beyond
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing! Wow, that was quite an excursion into what I thought was a simple subject covered in regular school Math. 🙂

If anyone is still confused, very simple way to remember this is using an example I learned in the very first SAP project. You're selling bottled drinks as single bottles and also cases of 12 bottles (typical case of 750 ml wine bottles). If a customer orders 15 bottles, you'll send them 1 full case (12 bottles) and 3 single bottles.

So, DIV (15,12) will give you number of full cases and MOD will give you number of additional single bottles. Cheers!

entry.title: Product Compliance (new): automatic substance checks not starting with uploaded materials
entry.content:
Dear Community

Issue with SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance (on-prem, release 2023), Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance solution capability.

Solution went live a month ago, with the first bundle of products/materials views (Raw and Unpacked) being created "by hand". 

As this process is very time consuming we are mass uploading materials data via Migration Cockpit. All is well with a major exception: the automatic substance checks, from the compliance requirements such as EU REACH SVHC Status, are not starting and thus, the compliance assessments are not performed.

I'm uploading the data with this sequence:

1. Bulk Raw Material Compliance Info -> 2. (related) Packaged Raw Material Compliance Info -> 3. (Bulk) Product Compliance Info -> 4. (related) Packaged Product Compliance Info (if necessary*) - 5. Analytical Composition 

* When uploading the Raw Material Compliance Info, if the logistic role of "sold" is marked, the Unpacked Compliance info is created.

For the time being we are letting out the materials that (will) have a material based composition (as these are few with my client).

Once again, I kindly ask for your advice and help

 

Paulo Pinto

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio?
entry.content:
No, ABAP applications cannot be directly developed in SAP Business Application Studio.

SAP Business Application Studio is primarily designed for developing modern applications based on SAP Fiori, SAP UI5, and SAP Cloud Platform technologies, focusing on front-end and cloud-based development. It's suited for building applications with JavaScript and other web development technologies.

For ABAP development, we typically uses SAP NetWeaver or ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse, which is the dedicated environment for ABAP programming. These tools are better suited for the server-side logic and the ABAP-based applications.

However, SAP Business Application Studio can be used to build applications that interact with ABAP systems via APIs or back-end services, but the actual ABAP coding itself is done in Eclipse or the SAP GUI-based ABAP Workbench.

Check out more here https://developers.sap.com/mission.cap-java-app.html

entry.title: Production Planning Master Data: Bill of Material
entry.content:
What is Bill of Material in Production Planning?

In a simple word, Bill of Material (BOM) is a structured list of all components (raw materials, semi-finished goods, finished goods) required to manufacture a finished product. It defines the relationship between a product and its components in Production Planning and Material Requirements Planning (MRP). BOM are use in several manufacturing types such as discrete manufacturing, process manufacturing, and repetitive manufacturing in SAP system.


Example of Bill of Material table


Example of Bill of Material structure

Bill of material is used to determine the required quantity of components (MRP), defines the production process and operations for the materials in the BOM, production order who used BOM to consume components in manufacturing, and also used to calculate the material costing. SAP will calculate the price for each material in the bill of material to be included in the cost of goods manufactured.

SAP supports various BOM types depending on the production and business requirements:

  • Material BOM – Used in production to define product structures.
  • Equipment BOM – Used in Plant Maintenance (PM) for maintenance planning.
  • Functional Location BOM – Used for maintenance at a specific location.
  • Sales Order BOM – Specific to customer requirements in Make-to-Order (MTO) scenarios.
  • Variant BOM – Supports configurable materials with different variations.
  • Multi-Level BOM – A hierarchical BOM where components also have their own sub-components.


Now let’s talk about how to create a BOM.

You can go to CS01 and insert the material, plant, and BOM usage. 
Then, go to the BOM header.

Choose the BOM status ‘1’ if you want to set the BOM active.

Then insert the components for this material. Also choose the item category and quantity.

So that's basically how you create a BOM. Feel free to discuss more if you have any other insights or questions!

A lifetime learner,
Dafina

entry.title: Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release – What’s New
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the innovations for Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. The What’s New presentation provides an additional overview of all innovations.  

The innovations are balanced across the areas core functionality, user experience, extensibility and integration, thus providing value additions for the whole portfolio. As always, this release has been a great team effort, and I am more than happy to dive right into it. 

 

CORE FUNCTION 

Lean Customer Returns 

The process Customer Returns (BKP) had already been enabled in 2208 with Warehouse Management and it covers the full customer returns process from receiving the materials to deciding on follow-up actions. Still, customers have been requesting us to also enable  Lean Customer Returns (BDD), since it is required for more special processes such as consignment pick-up as described in the process  Customer Consignment (1IU). With this release, such inbound deliveries can be received directly into Warehouse Management with document type Customer Returns Delivery (LR).

Figure 1 - App Change Inbound Deliveries 

Stock Transport Order (STO) with Split Valuation 

Split valuation enables customers to manage the same material with different values. Within a stock transport order, there is a special flag available on the stock transport order item: “GI-based GR”. When this flag is set, the valuation for the stock of the inbound delivery is calculated based on the outbound delivery. For this special scenario, the inbound deliveries can be received directly into Warehouse Management as of this release.

Figure 2 - App Change Purchase Order 

Outbound Delivery Order Split with Loading Completed 

With 2408, the outbound delivery order split had been introduced, allowing customers to split items or handling units from an outbound delivery order into a new outbound delivery order. However, that feature came with the restriction, that Transportation Management was out of scope. With 2502, we have followed up on this and enabled the process to also work with Transportation Management. Now, when the stop of the freight order is set to Loading Completed, all items and handling units which do not have the status Loaded set are split into a new outbound delivery order. In my view, this is a very important addition as this is the most relevant use case for an outbound delivery order split: Just during the loading process it is detected that not all handling units could be loaded onto the freight order and thus anything that cannot be loaded must be split into a new outbound delivery order to then be loaded onto the next freight order.

Figure 3 - App Load and Unload Freight Orders 

GS1 Barcode Support in RF 

GS1 barcodes are barcodes structured based on the specification defined by the GS1 organization. Due to this structure, one barcode can contain information on multiple entities such as GTIN number, SSCC number, batch number and many more. Usually, the shipper would print a shipping label for the pallet according to this barcode specification, so the receiver can scan the barcode and decode the different data points. As of this release, the RF transactions support the decoding of such barcodes. For example, in the RF transaction  Receive Handling Units by Delivery or ASN, if the user scans a GS1 decoded barcode which contains the GTIN, the quantity and the best-before date, all three values are automatically populated to the respective field on the screen.

  

Figure 4 - RF Transaction Receive Handling Units by Delivery or ASN 

Printer Determination for Handling Unit Label Printing Before Goods Receipt 

Often, the printer for printing a handling unit label is determined based on the storage bin of the handling unit. So far there has been a restriction, that this approach does not work if the handling unit is not yet posted goods receipt but only assigned to an inbound delivery with inbound delivery items packed into it because technically, the handling unit is not on a storage bin until it is posted goods receipt. To circumvent this dilemma, the goods movement bin of the inbound delivery item packed into the handling unit is now passed to the output parameter determination enabling the determination of the correction printer based on the storage bin also before goods receipt has been posted.

Figure 5 - App Output Parameter Determination 

 

USER EXPERIENCE 

Mass Item Change in the App Manage Outbound Delivery Orders 

Warehouse clerks occasionally need to update multiple items of an outbound delivery order at once, for example because all items need to be staged to a different staging bay or a different door. Updating the delivery items with a mass change has now been enabled, making it much more convenient for the end user to perform these changes. 

Figure 6 - App Manage Outbound Delivery Orders 

Schedule Inventory Document Creation – Cycle Counting 

Cycle counting is a process to continuously count certain products in certain intervals depending on their value. Therefore, the creation of the corresponding physical inventory documents should happen in background based on the cycle counting indicator stored on the warehouse product master data. By introducing the app Schedule Inventory Document Creation – Cycle Counting, these physical inventory documents can now be created in background instead of the user having to manually create them.

 

EXTENSIBILITY 

CDS Views 

There are multiple use cases for CDS views such as a data source for SQL selects, data source for defining custom CDS views or exposing them as OData service. With this release, CDS views have been released to make the data accessible for Fixed Bin Assignments, Alternative Handling Unit Identifications and Physical Inventory Documents. All of these cover the transactional use cases, while the CDS views for Physical Inventory Documents can also be used for analytical use cases.  

Transfer of Custom Fields for the Handling Unit Header 

Custom fields have already been enabled for the handling unit object in Handling Unit Management, but so far these could not be transferred to the handling unit in Warehouse Management. With the new release, these fields are now taken over from Handling Unit Management and can be displayed in Warehouse Management. 

 

Integration 

Warehouse Task Events 

When discussing with partners on project feedback, one thing that came up frequently is that while there are already many APIs available to build a custom logic to perform a certain action, quite often the timing on when to run the custom logic was an issue. Events are the answer to this challenge because they are raised directly upon a certain action and inform all subscribers immediately. We are therefore convinced that warehouse task events are a great addition to the toolbox, allowing partners to tie the custom logic to these events. An event will be raised whenever a warehouse task is created, confirmed, cancelled or activated.  

 

Figure 7 - Warehouse Task Events   

Additions to the Warehouse Order and Task API 

Not only have events for warehouse tasks been introduced but also the API has been improved. In this release, we have extended it by the following actions and parameters:  

  • Action ReassignToWarehouseOrder to assign warehouse tasks to a different warehouse order 
  • Action LockWarehouseOrder and UnlockWarehouseOrder to lock and unlock a warehouse order 
  • Optional parameter DirectWhseTaskConfIsAllowed to confirm a warehouse task without having to previously assign a resource to the warehouse order. This feature has been requested frequently as the restriction which requires to first assign a resource has been introduced for the context of mobile warehousing. In the meantime, the warehouse task APIs is used for many more use cases making this restriction often unnecessary. 
  • Optional parameters EWMWhseTskExcptnCodeChgBatch and Batch to  
    • ... confirm batch-specific warehouse tasks with a different batch using an exception code  
    • ... confirm batch-neutral warehouse tasks by specifying the batch upon confirmation 

Additions to the Physical Stock API 

The physical stock API has been extended by these six actions to cover all posting change scenarios and the most relevant ad-hoc goods issue scenarios:  

  • Consume stock 
  • Scrap stock 
  • Change sales order or project reference 
  • Change disposal party 
  • Change product or batch 
  • Change stock owner and usage 

FIND OUT MORE! 

There are more enhancements and details available in the What's new presentation.

entry.title: Public Sector Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
The 2502 release of S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition for public sector is here.

As planned, the Public Sector Management (PSM) Global scope bundle is now available for Austria, Japan, India, Singapore, New Zealand, Belgium, and Switzerland, alongside the existing countries: the US, Germany, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.  With this release, the US GASB scope is now also available as part of the PSM Global scope.   Moving forward with this streamlined release approach, allows for the continued expansion of the enterprise scope items also available to be deployed with PSM.   75 additional line of business scope items are added for selection with 2502, including the following:

  • IFRS – International Financial Reporting Standards
  • Intercompany functionality
  • Logistics – extended warehouse management
  • PO and service entry accruals
  • Sales management

With ever increasing demand, we continue the planned expansion to additional countries in 2508.  Check out the roadmap for details.  

With an expanded focus on AI capabilities, including the enhanced functionality of SAP Joule, our AI-powered co-pilot, organizations can leverage smarter, more intuitive tools to navigate a dynamic landscape with agility and confidence. SAP Joule now brings even more powerful insights, and delivers personalized, data-driven recommendations to staff working on projects, grants, budgets, and finance, enabling them to focus on strategic priorities rather than operational complexities.

 

Cloud ERP core enhancements

As always, there’s a range of new core Cloud ERP feature-based innovations for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the 2502 release. If you are interested in other areas of innovations for SAP’s Cloud ERP, please check out the Release Video from Bert Schulze:

Screenshot + URL: Get the top highlights of the 2502 release in this video by Bert Schulze

AI-Powered UX with SAP Joule

Joule, our Gen AI co-pilot, transforms user experience by simplifying tasks with instant insights and seamless navigation using natural language input. A standout feature is its personalized card creation, allowing users to quickly access critical ERP data like purchase orders and delivery updates, empowering more informed decision-making, and insights.  Joule provides navigational assistance to public sector users.   Where do I go to create a grant?  Show me a budget overview.   Not only does Joule help users quickly access the apps they need, but it also provides easy access to SAP’s available documentation.   What’s a sponsored program?  Tell me about grant billing.

Screenshot/Video URL: AI in Grants and Budget Management. 

As we advance in AI-assisted UX capabilities, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release brings exciting innovations in BETA testing, including:

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home
  • and AI-assisted error explanation

You can sign up through this link to start your BETA testing!

 
PSM Global

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2502 release delivers a robust suite of Grants and Budget Management features tailored for public sector entities, including state and local governments, non-profits, cities, K-12 schools, and education & research institutions.

Building upon previous releases, the 2502 update introduces a range of new capabilities designed to support public sector customers. Read on for an overview of the latest advancements.

For more details on this new global PSM scope bundle, visit the SAP Process Navigator to explore the complete list of available scope items. Direct link to the Roadmap Explorer: URL

Screenshot: Process Navigator focused on S/4HANA Public Cloud, and the PSM industry capabilities.


Residual-budget processing for Grants

Easily carry forward residual (unused) budget via the Budget Specialist Role into the next fiscal year:

  • Have the ability to reuse a residual budget in line with legislation
  • Improve reporting along fiscal years
  • Exhibit fiduciary use of taxpayer money through increased compliance and transparency of the budget lifecycle

It’s now possible to move residual budget of grants forward in line with the existing residual budget processing. 

Note: for external budget (Relevant grant/External Fund), it is not possible to change the budget period.

 Overview:

  • Enables either lapse or carry forward of unspent grant funds to the next fiscal year.
  • Ensures consistency by extending residual budget processing to grants, not just general budgets.
  • Maintains clear records of carried-forward amounts for transparency and reporting.

Benefits:

  • Prevents loss of unspent funds, optimizing resource allocation.
  • Provides better forecasting and control over multi-year grant spending.
  • Enable users to restrict future reporting of grant budgets on the current fiscal year vs reporting back into past fiscal years.

Screenshot: set up a residual budget transfer for Grants.

 

Screenshot: successful run of residual budget transfer process

Output services for Earmarked Funds

Earmarked Funds documents allow for the reservation of expenditure budget in advance of expected procurement activity or direct expenses or to provide visibility for expected revenue.  Output services allow for a preformatted output with options to email or print

Overview:

  • Easily create and export earmarked fund documents in a standardized PDF format.
  • Ensures earmarked fund records are stored electronically for easy retrieval and audit.
  • Generates legally compliant documents with necessary approvals and timestamps.

Benefits:

  • Ensures earmarked fund documents meet regulatory and audit requirements.
  • Enables quick access, retrieval, and long-term digital archiving of financial records.
  • Prevents document tampering and ensures authenticity with standardized PDF outputs.

Screenshot: creation of the Earmarked Fund and the PDF output document.

Screenshot: PDF output from the Earmarked Fund Document

Enhancements – Commitments Based on Due Date

The ability to consume the budget for an expected expense instead of the posting date was previously released.  New enhancements streamline and clarify these options.  When due date is selected the following apply:

  • if due date is empty in the document, the posting date acts as budget consumption date 
  • for commitment changes before the budget consumption/due date, the existing budget consumption date is used for the updates 
  • for changes after the budget consumption date, the logic for posting dates applies (CPU date or update date act as budget consumption date) 

 

Screenshot: define update date for budget consumption.

 

AVC activity groups for Travel Requests and Expense Report Obligation

 The availability control check (AVC check) compares the consumable budget with the consumed amounts (commitments and actual postings) for budget control objects.  Flexible, comprehensive AVC allows the customer to determine at what levels budget is checked, for which account assignments, and how the system responds. New AVC activity groups for travel requests and expense report obligations support flexibility for SAP Concur integration.

Note: it is not possible to run PSM AVC in parallel with project or cost center AVC. If PSM is active, only the PSM AVC can be used to control budget consumption.

 

Screenshot: tolerance profile activity groups

 

 Key Summary

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition latest 2502 release brings key enhancements, including AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, and improved Grants and Budget Management capabilities.  This release continues to drive efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in ERP operations for public sector organizations. Please join us in the Cloud ERP revolution and explore how these innovations can unlock your organization’s full potential.

 

Sneak peak into what’s next!

Want to try out S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, for public sector?   Get a closer look at budget and grants management scenarios?   Access to the new public trial system will soon be available.   Register for a two-week trial and take it for a spin.   Look for my next blog coming soon with the full announcement and link.

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21st and January 24th, we conducted a series of engaging live sessions to showcase the exciting innovations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

 Find All of Our Assets   

The 2502 release showcases 185 innovation highlights across the portfolio. 
Check out our release document for all the details!

Screenshot: Key Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Grievance Tracking Extension Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
1) Purpose of the Blog

The purpose of this blog is to highlight Tech Envo's Grievance Tracking Solution, designed to foster a transparent and compliant workplace by enabling employees to easily report concerns. Built with workplace compliance in mind, this solution streamlines grievance management, ensuring accountability, quick resolution, and improved organizational trust. By leveraging a user-friendly interface and robust tracking capabilities, the solution enhances transparency, reduces manual effort, and promotes a positive work environment. This blog aims to showcase how the solution addresses key challenges in grievance handling while empowering organizations to maintain a fair and compliant workplace.


 2) Architecture Design of the Grievance Tracking SAP BTP Extension Solution

 

The architecture of the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution is designed for efficiency, security, and seamless integration with SAP environments. The user interface, built with SAP UI5 Fiori Elements, provides a responsive and intuitive experience, allowing employees and HR administrators to easily navigate the grievance management system.

At its core, the backend leverages the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model and Node.js, ensuring robust data processing, workflow automation, and secure integration with SAP SuccessFactors through an SAP BTP destination. This architecture enables organizations to deploy the solution as a standalone application within SAP Build Work Zone or embed it as a portlet within SAP SuccessFactors, offering flexibility and scalability in grievance management.

Hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), the solution ensures data security, compliance, and uninterrupted access, empowering organizations to streamline grievance tracking, enhance transparency, and promote a fair workplace environment.

3)  What is the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution?


 Our powerful SaaS- based Grievance Tracking Extension Solution empowers HR administrators to efficiently manage and resolve employee concerns with ease. Designed to handle cases related to harassment, discipline, and general grievances, this solution streamlines the entire process—from submission to resolution. With an automated approval workflow, grievances are systematically reviewed by management and escalated to arbitration boards when necessary, ensuring a fair, transparent, and consistent resolution process. By fostering accountability and reinforcing your organization’s commitment to employee well-being, this solution helps create a more supportive and compliant workplace.

 

4)   How TechEnvo’s Grievance Tracking Extension Solution Benefits Organizations

  1. Effortless & Transparent Grievance Reporting
    Empower employees with a simple, intuitive grievance submission process—just a few clicks, and their concerns are officially recorded. Our automated workflows ensure swift processing, guaranteeing timely resolutions while maintaining complete transparency throughout the journey.


     

    2.Comprehensive Grievance Lifecycle Management
    From initiation to resolution, every grievance is meticulously documented within the system. Even after closure, all cases remain securely stored for easy retrieval, ensuring compliance, audit readiness, and accountability in every step.

     

    3. Flexible Access for Seamless Integration
     Tailored to fit your organization’s unique needs:
    • Standalone Application – Available within SAP Build Work Zone on SAP BTP for a seamless and adaptable experience.
    • SAP SuccessFactors Integration – Easily embedded as a portlet for organizations already leveraging SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring a smooth and uninterrupted workflow.


       4. Built-in Document Management for Streamlined Operations
           Effortlessly upload, store, and retrieve grievance-related documents within the application—no need    for external document management tools. Everything you need, all in one place.

 With our Grievance Tracking Extension Solution, transform the way your organization handles grievances—promoting fairness, transparency, and efficiency at every step.


5) Why Choose the Grievance Tracking Solution?

A workplace that listens is a workplace that thrives. Addressing employee grievances effectively is key to fostering a positive, productive, and compliant work environment.

 
  • End-to-End Grievance Management – Seamlessly create, track, and resolve grievance cases while involving union representatives for fair and structured dispute resolution. All related documents can be securely uploaded, ensuring a well-documented and transparent process.
  • Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) – Leverage the power of SAP BTP for a seamless, scalable, and high-performance solution that integrates effortlessly with existing SAP systems.
  • Seamless SAP SuccessFactors Integration – Organizations using SAP SuccessFactors can embed this solution directly into their workflows, providing a consistent and intuitive user experience.
  • Compliance & Workplace Excellence – Stay ahead of workplace regulations while cultivating an environment where employees feel heard, valued, and empowered to contribute their best.

     

    With TechEnvo’s Grievance Tracking Solution, organizations can proactively manage grievances, drive transparency, and build a culture of trust and accountability.

     

    6) Get Started with the Grievance Tracking Extension Solution

    The Grievance Tracking Extension Solution is now available to help organizations create a transparent, efficient, and structured approach to managing employee grievances. Powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), this solution simplifies grievance handling while ensuring compliance and accountability.

    With its seamless integration into SAP SuccessFactors or as a standalone application within SAP Build Work Zone, HR administrators can efficiently track, document, and resolve employee concerns in a secure and automated environment.

    🔹Enhance workplace transparency
    🔹Ensure compliance with labor regulations
    🔹Streamline grievance resolution with structured workflows

    Ready to transform your grievance management process? Contact us today to see how this solution can empower your workforce and strengthen your organization’s commitment to employee well-being!

entry.title: Automate Username Updates to Previous Employment Records
entry.content:
AD Writeback in Rehire With New Employment

Good day

Another interesting one for a bit of architecting.  In many hybrid implementations, rehire with new employment is quite a usual scenario.  With SSO / IAS / Authentication against Azure IdP (or whatever your particular scenario), you might have come across a scenario where the AD UPN / SamAccountName had been created on the now-terminated employment record’s username, and the new employment record’s username is the same as the new User ID.  So, today I will be looking at ways to be able to automate the process of updating the username for the previous employment, so the any automated processes that tries to write the same AD UPN/SamAccountName back to the new User ID, would succeed.

First, there are two scenarios in my particular example:  A permanent employee terminated in one Legal Entity to join another (typical of hybrid implementations), or when a contingent worker joins as a permanent employee.  The first can be achieved by leveraging the Intelligent Services Centre (ISC) event for terminations and an Integration Centre (IC) job.  Note, however, that when a Work Order ends, the Contingent becomes inactive, but a termination is not logged in ISC.

Permanent Employees

I have adapted my previous blog post logic that I previously wrote about updating the username for inactive employees here:  instead of scheduling a recurring job in Integration Centre (IC), I use the ISC Termination Event to trigger the update.  Also, instead of pre-pending the username with ‘i_’, I simply map the user ID to the Username – this will ensure that it always has a unique username without the need to worry about a second or third rehire with new employment.

In IC:

 

In ISC:

 

In this particular case, I chose to let the IC job update the username a day after the effective termination date, to prevent possible issues with logging on while still finishing off the last day of work.

Contingent Worker

As mentioned above, an ISC Termination event is not raised when a work order ends.  In this scenario, I have created an IC scheduled job, restricted to only update usernames for job info records where there is an ECWK event reason (which will be on the day that the contractor’s status will be inactive).

In IC:

The field mappings look exactly like those for permanent employees.  In addition, I have added the following filters:

 

 

In my particular scenario, I set up the schedule to trigger every two hours, 12 times a day to cover the 24-hour rolling period.  Depending on your own use case, you might decrease the frequency.

 

That’s it.  Now the username on the old user ID will be updated to the unique User ID, thus allowing and automated writeback processes to successfully write the username to the new User ID / Employment Record.

If this helps, give a kudo!  If not, well, feel free to reach out and I am happy to look at a different architecture that might work for you.

Regards

Eugene

entry.title: Process Order to warehouse order confirmation process
entry.content:
Process Order:
A process order in SAP EWM is used to manage the execution of production or manufacturing processes within the warehouse. It integrates with other SAP modules like SAP PP (Production Planning) to coordinate the movement of materials for production. SAP EWM helps optimize inventory handling and material flow based on the requirements of the process order.

PMR (Planned Movement Request):
PMR in SAP EWM is a request for the movement of goods based on planned requirements, such as production or transfer orders. It helps ensure the timely availability of materials by generating tasks for moving goods to designated locations. The PMR acts as a proactive plan to streamline warehouse operations and material handling.

Staging:
Staging in SAP EWM refers to the process of temporarily placing materials in a designated staging area before they are moved to the next processing stage, like shipping or production. It ensures that goods are readily available when needed, optimizing material flow. Staging areas are strategically managed to support efficient workflows and minimize delays.

Warehouse Task:
A warehouse task in SAP EWM is a specific action or instruction assigned to a worker, such as picking, putaway, or stock transfer. It is generated based on various warehouse processes like inbound or outbound deliveries. Warehouse tasks help organize and manage warehouse operations, ensuring that tasks are completed in an efficient and timely manner.

Warehouse Order:
A warehouse order in SAP EWM groups multiple warehouse tasks into a single unit, providing instructions for a worker to complete a set of related tasks. It optimizes task management by organizing work, increasing efficiency. Warehouse orders help streamline operations and ensure smooth task execution across the warehouse.

Steps for creation of the Process Order to warehouse order confirmation:

 

Step 1:

Create New Process order

Process order 1000 is created.

 

 

 

Step 2:

Check the PMR in Transaction /N/SCWM/PMR

Process order 1000

 

 

 

 

Step 3:

Perform Staging in Transaction /N/SCWM/STAGE

 

 

 

 

 

Step 4:

Check the Warehouse order linked to Process order

 

 

Warehouse order 2000 is linked to Process order 1000

 

Confirm the warehouse order from the SAP monitor (/n/scwm/mon) else the warehouse confirmation transaction code /n/scwm/to_conf

 

Conclusion:

 

Steps for creation of the Process Order to warehouse order confirmation is provided.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: ESRS S1-6 – Characteristics of the undertaking’s employees
entry.content:
Disclaimer: This blog post reflects the state of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) as of February 11th, 2025.

ESRS S1-6 requires a company to disclose quantitative and qualitative (/narrative) information about its employees. SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) provides out of the box several quantitative metrics, hence this article focusses on how to bridge the gap for the ESRS S1-6 requirements for the quantitative data points. Specifically, the focus is on paragraph 50 a: "the undertaking shall disclose the total number of employees by head count, and breakdowns by gender and by country for countries in which the undertaking has 50 or more employees representing at least 10% of its total number of employees." 

Total Number of Employees

For the “total number of employees by head count” the SAP provided metric Total Number of Employees can be used.  

Breakdown by Gender

The breakdown by gender is currently not out of the box possible in SCT. In the additional application requirement 55 (AR 55) it is described that the company shall disclose the information in a tabular format and provides the following template:

As of now SCT doesn’t allow to display tables. However, it is possible to visualize the breakdown by gender with Standard Custom Metrics. You can create a Standard Custom Metric within the Manage Metrics app:

Then you can define the details of the Standard Custom Metric. In this case, for Z_1-16_FEMALE:

Please ensure that your leading and supported reporting hierarchies are set correctly.

I.e., if you provide information via the Measure “Workforce Headcount” and its template “Workforce Headcount – Actuals” in the Manage ESG Data app, you need to ensure that the respective hierarchies are maintained if you want them to be supported in SCT.

  • ID_COMPANY_CODE stands for Legal Entity
  • ID_ORG_UNIT stands for Reporting Line
  • ID_BUSINESS_LOC stands for Business Location

In the example above all hierarchies are provided with data and hence should be supported.

In the calculation definition you can select the Measure “Workforce Headcount”, the Calculation Method is Sum, and you select the respective filter to sum up. In this case you sum up all entries with Gender Female.  As the requirement is to disclose the headcount it is crucial to set the Value Type to Row Count.

Background: In your template you define both, MEASURE_VALUE_CHAR and MEASURE_COUNT. For the “Workforce Headcount” the former defines the full time equivalent (FTE) of each entry whereas the latter defines the headcount. In this simplified example there are in total 275 FTEs and the headcount is 350. This can be the case due to part time working employees. When defining the calculation in SCT the value type defines whether is should sum up the aggregated values (in this case the MEASURE_VALUE_CHAR – FTE) or the rows (in this case MEASURE_COUNT – headcount).

After creating the Standard Custom Metric and uploading the “Workforce Headcount – Actuals” template in the Manage ESG Data app, you can then calculate the data for the Standard Custom Metrics, and publish it. E.g., for Z_S1-6_FEMALE:

After the calculation you can already see that SCT selected the correct entries to be summed up:

In the Analyze ESG Data app you can the visualize all calculated Standard Metrics and SAP provided Metrics:

Breakdown by country for countries in which the undertaking has 50 or more employees representing at least 10% of its total number of employees.

The breakdown by country with the respective filtering of 50+ employees and representing at least 10% of the company’s total number of employees is currently not out of the box possible in SCT. In the additional application requirement 55 (AR 55) it is described that the company shall disclose the information in a tabular format and provides the following template:

As of now SCT doesn’t allow to display tables. However, it is possible to visualize the breakdown by countries with the Our Ambitions app. If you drill down the Total Number of Employees Metric under the Business Location hierarchy, you can visualize the breakdown per country. Please note that this requires the appropriate set-up of the master data and the provided actuals. Furthermore, a filtering of 50+ employees and representing at least 10% of the company’s total number of employees is also here not possible.
